rulings.Case Title: BAOLAI TANG VS. LAZ PARKING CALIFORNIA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 6, 2020, Line 3, DEFENDANT LAZ PARKING CALIFORNIA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication. Defendant Laz Parking California, LLC's motion for summary judgment is granted. Laz Parking does not have a general duty to safeguard plaintiff's property; therefore, plaintiff cannot establish its prima facie case for premises liability or negligence. In the commercial property context, absent an express covenant on the point or the landlord's voluntarily undertaking to protect a tenant's property, landlords do not owe a duty to safeguard tenant property from reasonably foreseeable criminal activity (burglary). (Royal Neckwear Co., Inc. v. Century City, Inc. (1988) 205 Cal.App.3d 1146, 1151; see Rotman v. Maclin Markets, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1709, 1715 [lessor not liable for theft of tenant's jewelry merchandise in market rental space because no agreement to provide such security and no evidence lessor voluntarily assumed such duty].) Plaintiff attempts to argue that Laz Parking has a duty to maintain and operate the garage in a reasonable manner, following certain protocols. (Opposition 4:19-22; 5:6-11; 6:9-13.) However, it is undisputed that plaintiff does not have a contract with defendant Laz (UMF #2) and there are no facts or evidence that suggests Laz Parking assumed a duty to protect plaintiff's property. Plaintiff's request for a continuance pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 437c(h) is denied. Plaintiff's counsel's declaration does not demonstrate that a continuance is needed in order to obtain facts to oppose this motion, specifically to the issue of duty. (Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389, 395.) Instead, counsel's declaration asserts that further discovery may yield evidence that Laz's employee is somehow responsible for the burglary, thus necessitating plaintiff to add the employee as a DOE defendant in which Laz Parking may be vicariously liable and/or liable for negligent hiring and supervision. (Yu Decl., Paras. 2-4.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL VS. ABDULBASET AHMED HUBAISHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Al-Hubaishy To Appear For Deposition And For Him Or His Attorney To Reimburse The City For Costs For His Failure To Appear At First Noticed Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 6, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Al-Hubaishy To Appear For Deposition And For Him Or His Attorney To Reimburse The City For Costs For His Failure To Appear At First Noticed Deposition Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted: Defendant Abdulbaset Ahmed Hubaishy ("Al-Hubaishy") shall appear for his deposition within ten days or as otherwise noticed by the City. Defendant, but not his attorney, shall reimburse the City for costs totaling $2,304.20 for his failure to appear at his first noticed deposition. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KHEHRA DENTAL CORPORATION, ET AL VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 6, 2020, Line 3, 1-PLAINTIFFs KHEHRA DENTAL CORPORATION and HARJIT KHEHRA D.D.S.' Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address the following subjects, among others: If Delta Dental's Motion for a protective order as to the second set of discovery is granted, what effect does that have on the instant motion as to UCL (B&PC 17200) discovery? Does the UCL (B&PC 17200), or cases interpreting it, limit discovery regarding unnamed persons of interest (i.e. the UCL equivalent of unnamed class members)? What is the import of the statute of limitations on the scope of discovery here? Are the answers to Special Interrogatories 28-29 equally available to Plaintiff? If not, why not? As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA RUIZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant City And County Of San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 6, 2020, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PATRICIA RUIZ's Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: The City's objections on the basis of privacy are denied. Subject to the foregoing, the parties shall meet and confer in an attempt to more narrowly tailor the document requests. In general, the City must produce all documents from the SFMTA that relate in any way to the causes of action alleged. They need not produce such documents as to every citation Plaintiff has ever written. The Motion is continued until September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. If the parties resolve these issues before that date, please inform the court and the hearing will be taken off calendar. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
